steps.narrate: Morning came thin and grey. The ash had stopped sometime in the dark, and the forest looked, almost, like a forest again.
Almost. The trees still leaned toward something he could not see. The path under his boots remembered other footsteps.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: Bureau patrols start at noon. We have until then.

steps.narrate: He looked at the clearing. Three things in his memory pulled at him before the fourth — the path forward.

choice.text: Approach the small shrine in the bushes.

steps.narrate: [ Filler — the choice block above always jumps past this. ]
A tree stump, old enough that its rings had turned to stone.
Someone had carved letters into it once. The rain had eaten most of them, but two remained: A. E.
His hand moved toward the carving before he could stop it.
But his fingers traced the grooves one more time before he pulled away.
A small shrine, half-swallowed by bushes. Smooth stones stacked into a cairn no taller than his knee.
Wax had pooled at the base — old, candle after candle, year after year. Someone had been keeping vigil here.
On top of the cairn, a single brass token. A name engraved, worn nearly smooth. He could not read it.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: Burners come here. The ones who don't have a body to bury.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: They leave a name. Sometimes their own.

steps.narrate: He did not leave a token. He did not have one to spare.
Elia waited where the path forked, hands loose at her sides, watching him in that careful way she had.
He didn't answer. She did not push.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: Whatever happens out there — you don't have to burn anything else today.
steps.distorted_text: Whatever happens out there — try not to burn the rest of yourself.

steps.narrate: She nodded, once, and looked away.
The path narrowed. The trees pressed closer.
Something had been here. The ground was wrong — patches of moss bleached white, bark stripped in long parallel grooves.
Ahead, where the canopy opened, something exhaled.
Not breath. Something that wanted to be breath but had forgotten how.

steps.speaker: Elia
steps.text: It's between us and the road. We can't go around.

steps.narrate: He drew his sword. The grip remembered his hand, even when he did not always remember the grip.
